video_subject: Iran-US Peace Deal: What It Means for the Forex Market
video_script: A surprise breakthrough in the Middle East is sending shockwaves through the currency markets. The United States and Iran have signed a framework to end their war, and the deal took effect immediately. Crude oil prices crashed nearly five percent, with US crude closing just above eighty dollars a barrel. At the heart of the deal, Iran agreed to instantly reopen the Strait of Hormuz, restoring one of the world's most vital oil shipping routes. For forex traders, the message was clear: the safe-haven rush into the US dollar has started to unwind. As war fears faded, riskier currencies like the euro and the Australian dollar pushed higher. Falling oil also eased inflation pressure, giving central banks more room to consider rate cuts. But analysts warn the calm may be fragile, with real doubts over whether Hormuz stays open for good. Commodity-linked currencies, from the Canadian dollar to the Norwegian krone, are now swinging with every oil headline. For the forex market, a single signature has rewritten the global outlook overnight.
